description.en: The size of the decompressed contents of the frame.

excerpt.es: The size of the decompressed contents of the frame.
excerpt.en: The size of the decompressed contents of the frame.

description.en: The content of the Zstandard dictionary, a bytes object. It’s the same as the dict_content argument in the __init__ method. It can be used with other programs, such as the zstd CLI program.

excerpt.es: The content of the Zstandard dictionary, a bytes object. It’s the same as the dict_content argument in the __init__ method. It can be used with other programs, such as the zstd CLI program.
excerpt.en: The content of the Zstandard dictionary, a bytes object. It’s the same as the dict_content argument in the __init__ method. It can be used with other programs, such as the zstd CLI program.

description.en: Identifier of the Zstandard dictionary, a non-negative int value. Non-zero means the dictionary is ordinary, created by Zstandard functions and following the Zstandard format. 0 means a “raw content” dictionary, free of any format restriction, used for advanced users. Note The meaning of 0 for ZstdDict.dict_id is different from the dictionary_id attribute to the get_frame_info() function.

description.en: When compressing with a ZstdDict, the dictionary’s ID is written into the frame header. True or 1 enable storing the dictionary ID while False or 0 disable it.

excerpt.es: When compressing with a ZstdDict, the dictionary’s ID is written into the frame header. True or 1 enable storing the dictionary ID while False or 0 disable it.
excerpt.en: When compressing with a ZstdDict, the dictionary’s ID is written into the frame header. True or 1 enable storing the dictionary ID while False or 0 disable it.

description.en: An integer representing the Zstandard dictionary ID needed for decompressing the frame. 0 means the dictionary ID was not recorded in the frame header. This may mean that a Zstandard dictionary is not needed, or that the ID of a required dictionary was not recorded.

description.en: An IntEnum containing the advanced decompression parameter keys that can be used when decompressing data. Parameters are optional; any omitted parameter will have it’s value selected automatically. The bounds() method can be used on any attribute to get the valid values for that parameter. Example setting the window_log_max to the maximum size: data = compress(b'Some very long buffer of bytes...') _lower, upper = DecompressionParameter.window_log_max.bounds() options = {DecompressionParameter...

description.en: Decompress data (a bytes-like object), returning the uncompressed data as a bytes object. The options argument is a Python dictionary containing advanced decompression parameters. The valid keys and values for compression parameters are documented as part of the DecompressionParameter documentation. The zstd_dict argument is an instance of ZstdDict containing trained data used during compression. This must be the same Zstandard dictionary used during compression. description.en: Decompress data (a bytes-like object), returning uncompressed data as bytes. Some of data may be buffered internally, for use in later calls to decompress(). The returned data should be concatenated with the output of any previous calls to decompress(). If max_length is non-negative, the method returns at most max_length bytes of decompressed data.
